hawkish_phrases: the conditioning assumption underpinning the February projections – that there will be some modest withdrawal of monetary stimulus over the course of the forecast period – remains appropriate
if aggregate demand growth remains resilient, monetary policy may need to be tightened sooner and to a greater degree than that implied path
Kristin Forbes considered it appropriate to increase Bank Rate by 25 basis points
there are limits to the extent that above-target inflation can be tolerated
CPI inflation increased to 1.8% in January, and the MPC expects it to rise above the 2% target over the next few months, before peaking at around 2¾% in early 2018
dovish_phrases: the Committee expects a slowdown in aggregate demand over the course of this year, as household demand growth declines in reaction to lower real income growth

Monetary policy cannot prevent either the real adjustment that is necessary as the UK moves towards its new international trading arrangements or the weaker real income growth that is likely to accompany it
the MPC continued to judge that it remained appropriate to seek to return inflation to the target over a somewhat longer period than usual
A more marked slowdown in activity than currently anticipated by the Committee, by contrast, could warrant additional policy support
summary: The MPC voted 8-1 to hold Bank Rate at 0.25% with one dissenter favoring a 25bp hike, while forward guidance explicitly assumes some modest withdrawal of monetary stimulus over the forecast period and signals tightening may be needed sooner if demand remains resilient.
